description:
Goal
Restore the intended `SealSemanticClosure` settled-read contract without causing backend/native re-demand churn. Today the job reports settled reads to the scheduler, but several inner checks still use current fact presence/revision. A direct cleanup to `fact_is_settled` makes `compiler2_backend_program_revision_stays_stable_for_identical_recompute` and related quicksort artifact tests spin or timeout, which means the semantic frontier is not yet publication-stable enough for the honest settled contract.

Signal
- A red test first captures the failure: changing `SealSemanticClosure` to require settled `Activation`, `ActivationAnalyzed`, `ReturnType`, `LoweredBody`, and `CallSiteSummary` facts causes repeated backend/materialization work or drive timeout on quicksort re-demand.
- After the fix, `SealSemanticClosure` checks the same readiness it reports in `JobEffects` for every semantic prerequisite; no current-read/settled-use mismatch remains.
- Backend, emission, materialization, native, and AOT/JIT front-door re-demand tests pass under hard external timeouts.
- The fix uses dynamic activation-cycle convergence and stored-state publication, not a looser seal check or a timeout increase.

Strategy
- Land only after `fz-rh2.18.9.7` and `fz-rh2.18.9.8` make cyclic semantic facts monotone and output projection stable.
- Reapply the minimal settled-read cleanup in `jobs/root.rs` as the red test shape, then fix the underlying dirty/republication cycle rather than weakening the seal.
- Use telemetry from the timeout case to identify which facts keep dirtying `SealSemanticClosure` and which publisher retracts/reintroduces them.
- Keep artifact jobs waiting on `Settled(SemanticClosed(root))`; the seal is the place that must become honest.

Outro
No current-read/settled-use mismatch in `SealSemanticClosure`, no timeout increase hiding the churn, no artifact job polling revisions manually, no reverting to `DependencySnapshot`/`semantic_closure_is_current`, and no accepting backend re-demand hangs as a known limitation.
title: Compiler2 semantic closure: restore honest settled reads without re-demand churn
